title: 'The masters of AI will be hated for their wealth and their transformation of society'
subhead: A sign of the extraordinary vitality of American capitalism, artificial intelligence – led by its new masters SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI – could become the next black swan of the global economy, writes Le Monde's Arnaud Leparmentier in his column.

Discontent with Putin rises among part of the Russian population: 'There is an immense wall between you and us'
subhead: Internet shutdowns, a struggling economy and the ongoing war in Ukraine have exasperated some Russians, while a recent poll shows the president's popularity is slipping.

title: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
subhead: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.

title: Louvre showcases 'beautiful unknown' Martin Schongauer, who influenced Michelangelo and Rembrandt
subhead: An outstanding exhibition brings together around 100 works by the engraver and painter, who was born in Colmar around 1445, all showcasing his exceptional mastery.

title: 'Why am I alive?': The Yazidi genocide as told by one of its survivors
subhead: Samih Taha, now a refugee in France, is one of the few survivors of the Islamic State group massacre of his village in northern Iraq on August 15, 2014. Then 17 years old, he went into debt to buy back his mother and sisters, who had been forced into sexual slavery. He told Le Monde his story and the tragedy that befell his family.

France's print media in crisis amid massive layoffs
subhead: Since December 2025, magazine and regional press companies have announced nearly 1,000 job cuts, putting the viability of countless newsrooms at risk.

title: Nathalie Baye, acclaimed French actress with vast filmography
subhead: Throughout her career, Baye intentionally took on a wide range of lead and supporting roles, portraying upper-class women to sex workers in both comedies and romantic dramas. She died on Friday at the age of 77 after a battle with Lewy body disease.

title: Ireland, the weak link in Europe's defense
subhead: Irish neutrality, long seen as a guarantee of independence from its former British colonizer, has been eroding under European and Atlantic pressures. After decades of naval weakness, Dublin has committed to strengthening its navy.
